author: xrs_01
content:
That makes sense, thank you. I'll definitely work on a more technical and practical research post to develop the idea further; the original post definitely reads more like a manifesto. I think the most intuitive way to understand it is by coming to the conclusion that the current architecture of 1 block-builder per slot is far from desirable in a decentralized network. Even on a basic level, if you did something like have 4 builders per block that execute disjointed (by interactions with state) parts of an inclusion list that seems beneficial.

One analogy is that single slot finality would end up being equivelant to an army of archers (validators) shooting at a single tree (block) within a vast forest of potential execution capacity (more trees) across the network. I personally think that monolithic execution is just a strange fallacy on-par with the famous "mountain man fantasy" of having every node re-verify the history; I hope that's not too controversial to say. 

Thank you very much for engaging with the idea as it's been hard to get feedback!
